channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 122 replies — 357.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 122 • Updated: 2026-07-05 08:07 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445575
>>62445384
Ah, the same 'rich and retired' guy who says he checks these threads once every few months?

Or a different 'rich and retired' guy?

Or larping as one?

How many rich and retired guys are there monitoring a Mongol basket weaving forum?

62445576
>>62445563
That's fine with me. I suspect that the warrants were a spit in the eye for hedgies who likely need to allow the price to run occasionally while they reload their ETFs and roll over their swaps. Putting a ceiling on how high they can let the price run is probably a factor they'd prefer to not have to deal with. The warrants, I suspect, will be one of those trades staring us all right in the face that no one is taking advantage of. I do tend to believe that this is a relevant timeframe that is well thought out in advance to account for the post-3 year crossing of the change in ownership to preserve the NOLs and likely foreshadowing the M&As that ultimately bring about Teddy. An insane inflow of capital is going to open the hedgie's anus as RC goes on a buying spree.

62445583
>>62445569
Jesus Christ he looks wrecked and miserable, the towel has taken a toll on him

62445599
>>62445569
>PLEASE GARY STOP THE TRADING IN THIS WORTHLESS NAME!

62445600
>>62445583
The dangers of leveraged trading are real. I think short sellers naturally formed a cabal as their interests were naturally all in line and they do have strength in numbers. They got too cocky and never really had a gameplan to counter the fact that someone could actually show up and want to turn a company around. Short sellers who try to bite off more than they can chew are like snakes who try to swallow prey that is too big and it gets stuck in their throats, unable to swallow. They inadvertently create high-value deals for someone like RC. They find themselves trapped in a turnaround story with no way to exit the trade. And with retail eyes all over the play, they really can't try to negotiate their way out of it. Doug looks so rough here because even the DTCC has been ousted in the fraud. So this is a complete embarrassment for the U.S. capital markets, and we even saw Tricia Rothschild come out from behind the curtain to help conceal the systemic naked short selling scheme.
It takes a lot of time and capital to create billions of dollars. You can't just put a company together overnight. You can, however, short sell a multi billion dollar company into the ground and STEAL all of the equity for yourself through counterfeiting quite easily. That's why they do it. Unfortunately for them, naked shorting is a trading strategy that exposes you to losses that can far exceed the amount of the original investment/exposure.

62445619
Take your meds you retarded schizo

62445628
>>62445569
Amy's blowjobs must suck

62445645
>>62445619
ABC had a space last week that had 500+ people watching. I'd imagine at least 10% of them were hedgies, analysts and silly shillies. It's a huge undertaking for retail to sit down and parse through these dockets to figure out what is happening. Wallstreet is well versed in this shit and have a dedicated team who analyzes the happenings of every stock out there. It's no surprise that Cifu started crying on Twitter because once he saw they were outflanked he knew immediately that the game was over and he blew his company up. They were giving money away for pennies because the barcode algo was taking the price to zero and was not phased in any way to price in what was about to happen. If the price was allowed to run back in February 6 2023, who knows where we could have ended up. Someone was getting squeezed back then and they decided to double, triple, quadruple down on the play because they were already bust. BBBYQ was already infiltrated and sabotaged and the short interests were already well outside any semblance of prudence by risk management standards. All that was left to do was to physically short the stock with impunity until it was near $0 and hope the debts were too much for anyone to try to buy them up.
But they were too little too late.

62445678
GUYS RYAN COHEN JUST REPLIED TO MIKEHANDS ON X. MIKEHANDS IS FAMOUS FOR BEING A REGULAR ON THEPPSHOW. RYAN LITERALLY CONFIRMED TODAY THAT WE WON. HOLY FUCKING SHIT

62445683
>>62445678

62445686
ABC is such a gargantuan retard, don't give him the credit of "parsing documents." He literally reads off an AI script verbatim and just rehashes the same theory everyone else is already aware of. He also tried to pivot into being this startup business X account and had to stop that nonsense fast when he realized how literally no one wants to listen to his mindless drivel. Now he's trying to be the mysterious "I know something you don't" type guy with a Carl Icahn profile picture, because those two are totally the same person.

God, I hate everyone in this play so fucking much. PP's also a massive retard, but at least that's his brand, and it's somewhat upfront.

62445700
>>62445686
Now that's what I call a Silly Shilly!

62445705
>>62445700
im not even a shill, as i also contemplate the complications of the CUSIP Conundrum. i even emails Cusip Global Services about it in March and they never got back to me--how very curious. that said, i am so sick of these retards on twitter pretending like they're doing anything but peddling false hype. they don't even bring up the CUSIP-6 thing which is fucking insane to me because that is a piece of evidence staring us right in the face for months now which has very interesting implications.

62445706
>>62445686
ABC? You mean Alfred?

62445735
>>62445619
relax.
He does software engineering (hes actually been stuck in tech support for 6 years).

He can hack you (him hacking you is just making shit up and alphastate reposting it)

62445743
>>62445686
Jake already confirmed we won, so it doesn't matter what anyone else says.

62445859
>>62445743
did anyone else itt literally cum when they saw that picture of him on the couch

62445900
>>62445859
Yea but like, most of us cum every day at least.

62445941
>>62445705
A silly shilly
Being a seethy weethy
Built for GME

62446010
>>62445859
I'm going to frame the picture of Jake on the couch and put it up in my mansion. When guests will ask who he is, I'll smirk and say "you wouldn't get it."

62446088
>>62445569

62446106
>>62445576
>>62445600
>>62445645

spot on

62446149
>>62446088
They leave trading open so smart people can sell "for tax purposes" and get the loss on their tax return. I guess they think idiots buying 7 biĺion worthless securities for 8 cents each is funny,,,,,i do

62446238
>>62445569
what is this image even from?

62446242
>>62446238
wait I found it:

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242
<suspended


Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

item.title: Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-07-05 08:21 UTC

OP

Post country and how much PTO you get
>aus
>5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Early replies (first 3)

62443952
US
"Flexible" but I took seven weeks last year

62443958
>>62443952

If you’re white collar professional is the USA really a nightmare for employees like everyone says ? Or it’s over exaggerated?

62443972
>>62443958
Depends on the company I guess. I've always imagined that your average retard that complains about a lack of PTO works fast food, retail, or something equally menial

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444027
>burger
>3.5 weeks

62444080
>>62443950
>US
>32 days
All PTO is lumped into one pool: sick days, holidays, vacation, same shit.

62444094
>>62444080

That’s great! I’d much rather have combined PTO than the Aus system where sick leave is seperate, since I never use it all. What do you do for work?

62444108
>>62444094
Structural engineer. Only people who have been with the company for ten years+ get that much PTO.

62444126
>>62443950
Us government

>~2 weeks sick paid
>~2 weeks annual leave paid
>Every federal holiday off
>3 months paid paternity leave (expires a year after birth)
>1 year "family leave" unpaid

62444133
America

I don't go to work I just sell shit online and gamble on lines

62444135
>>62443950
12 sick, 15 PTO, then we get 9 federal holidays, Christmas eve and my birthday off. Then on a de facto basis via declarations we normally get black friday and half days on the day before Thanksgiving and Christmas eve. So 38-40 days off a year.

>US state public employee

62444144
>>62443950
US
28 days ATO (PTO and sick leave)
it doesn't matter though, i'm paid strictly on productivity.

62444155
>>62443950
Canada
3 weeks PTO :(
11 days sick
2 family leave days

62444271
>>62443950
NZ
>2 weeks PTO.
>2 weeks Sick.
>4 weeks domestic abuse leave.
Bit awkward ringing the boss and telling him I can't come in because I'm beating his daughter, but such is life.

62444329
>>62443950
USA.
Unlimited PTO
Unlimited sick time
You'll get fired for being cheeky with it, but having a woman dedicated to tracking your vacation/holiday time and whether or not your sick is extremely low-trust society tier behavior. Spiritually canadian.

62444336
>>62443950
I get 2 weeks in canada
last year I forgot to use them

62444340
France
7 weeks paid leave

62444341
>>62443950
>US
>2.5 weeks vacation
>1 week "personal business"
>4 weeks sick

62444369
>>62443950
USA
>2 weeks sick
>4 weeks vac

62444406
>>62444155

Canada seems like a worse version of Australia no offence lol

62444407
>>62444271
Nz is 4 weeks annual leave right?

62444410
>>62444329

Don’t companies give unlimited PTO because the research has shown it causes employees to take less time off, not more? Since most want to Err on the side of caution.

Also how does it work if you leave the company? Here we get paid out our leave (not sick tho) in cash when we quit.

62444855
>>62443950
USA
25 days

29 next year.

62444899
>>62443950
USA 52 weeks. Get rich and own your time bitches

62444988
>>62444094
>since I never use it all
Why not? Don't sleep great one night and just take a sick day the next. Not abusing sick days is cucked

62445023
ItalyAfrica.
3 weeks

62445041
>>62444988

Yea I’m planning to, will just keep 5 days spare just in case

62446526
>>62443950
>Canada
>I work contract so maybe only work 6-8 months of the year then 4 months off with employment insurance but I've had my EI case open for like 5 years now, I just claim it every chance I get.

62446702
15 days per year of combined PTO + sick time as a burger tech worker. It was 13 days per year until I hit one year at the company.
I think I need to get a new job.

62447281
>>62443950
>Aus
>0 days annual leave
>0 days sick leave
>Unpaid public holidays
>No legal protections if they sack me
The advantage is that I get paid more than my colleagues, and I'm well-known enough in my industry that I'll never be out of work.

62447298
>>62443950
Latvia.
32 days plus unlimited sick leave and also I cam take paid sick leave to take care of my kids if they get sick.

62447300
>>62443958
You might have a huge salary but only 10 to 15 days paid time off and 0 sick leave. That's normal in America. And people are encouraged strongly to take 0 vacation days (can be "cashed out").

62447301
>>62444080
>holidays
Nobody was talking about holidays and now you're coping.
If the rest of us add in Holidays then we get like 50 days PTO a year.

62447310
Notice how America is right in-between or on par with most European countries, yet the doomer grifters on YouTube are always pushing shit about how Americans don't get any days off.

Same with the retarded air-conditioning debate. Now that Europeans are actually visiting Florida and Texas, they're like "oh, sorry Americans, we didn't realize you live in a fucking jungle hellscape".

item.title: House buying — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-05 08:27 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447266
>>62447246
1. Don't buy unless you're going to live there for 5 or 10 years, the break even is close to 15 years most places
2. Buying a house is the best thing I ever did because it forced me to pay into equity even when I couldn't afford to invest
3. Nobody knows how the market will go for houses. There's no guarantee they'll keep going up

62447271
>>62447266
What do you mean by break even, like if you were to sell it earlier than 15 years you wouldn't make any money back?

That's the main reason I haven't bought a house. I'm afraid I'll have to replace the roof and plumbing and wiring and shit then when I go to sell it i'll actually be in the hole for $50k.

Mortgageoids always make it seem like some magical thing where you flip the house for $100k any time you want.

62447273
>>62447257
>but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything
Amateur

62447280
>>62447271
Yep you're just paying interest for the first 5 years or so and even by 10 years you will have paid almost all interest and very little principle

So if you move and the house hasn't gone up much you may have only paid interest and lost money. Though to be fair if you rent you also lost money

62447282
>>62447280
If i did buy a house i would sell my stox and put $200k down. Idk how much that changes the calculations.

62447284
>>62447282
You're unlikely to lose your down payment but you would very likely lose all your payments because they're straight interest

Though again this is the same as renting. You lose your rent also

62447286
>>62447246
You should buy the most expensive real estate possible as early in life as possible. Trade up after equity and price gains.
Repeat.
Boomers were always right about this.
My brother and his wife bought their first house for like 225k around age 26.
2nd house for 425k around 31.
2nd house is worth 900k now.
They sold and relocated far away and bought a McMansion for 600k using gains from the 2nd house.

62447288
>>62447284
Just pay cash. You get to live there and the imputed rent is taxfree

62447289
>>62447266
>There's no guarantee they'll keep going up
They will. The entire economic system is entirely based on everything going up forever.

62447294
>>62447289
I've seen my house go down and stay down for years on 2 different occasions. Once in 2008-2011 and the other one is right now, the last few years.

62447316
>>62447286
Do $225k houses (not in the ghetto) even exist in the united states anymore?

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 105 replies — 44.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-07-05 06:04 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62439758
>>62438074
With that risk model, I don't think anyone needs to hand over their custody.

Self custody is part of what it's designed for.

62440739
>>62439758
what about in an exchange like binance

62441904
>>62439167
Maybe they have more coming that we just don't know about yet. It's actually a good sign that they're still building despite the current market conditions.

62442213
>>62440739
Kraken

62442214
>>62426512
Buy the dip, obviously.

62442227
>>62439167
But that won't help the price up

62442228
>>62442214
Now???

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

item.title: Watch this shit right the fuck now. I normally watch shit like and I always find stuff that's wrong or biased or it's cl… — 9 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Watch this shit right the fuck now. I normally watch shit like and I always find stuff that's wrong or biased or it's cl…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-05 07:54 UTC

OP

Watch this shit right the fuck now.

I normally watch shit like and I always find stuff that's wrong or biased or it's clear that the author doesn't really understand certain things about crypto or markets but HOLY SHIT EVERYTHING IS GREAT. I couldn't find anything that was blatantly biased or seriously wrong. To be perfectly honest, I was doing something while this was playing in the background, so I intend to watch it again later, but I could not find anything wrong. Can you? Post in this thread if you love it or hate it. I want to hear your genuine thoughts even though I think most of you are fucking retarded.

Early replies (first 3)

62447224
>>62447222
Holy shit I forgot to post the link. Maybe the real retard was me all along...

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eyyDNAj6K_c&t=2s

62447225
>>62447224
buy an ad faggot

62447226
>>62447224
can you summarize it? if he thinks it will go up then he's based and bullpilled. if he thinks it will go down then he's a gay beartard

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447229
>>62447225
Say that to my face fucker not online and see what happens. I didn't make the video, I just thought it was good.

>>62447226
I know it's really long like 18 min but he talks a lot about the recent crash from $80k to now and he also talks about how large institutions like Blackrock and Fidelity influence public perceptions for their own gain (not a big secret). He talks about the psychology of fear and how its used to attract exit liquidity and how the purchases made by large players is essentially locked away, never again destined to circulate freely so in practice the 1 million BTC held by investment firms is really out of circulation. He said a lot more. Just watch it or at least treat it as a podcast and listen to it since the animations are supplementary and it's really like a podcast anyway.

62447249
very organic thread, this.

62447256
>>62447249
You're a fucking nigger. How's that for organic faggot? I genuinely wanted to share this with you and I wanted your opinion but it seems like you're too much of a pussy. Fuck you. If ever saw you irl I would knock you the fuck out pussy.

62447258
>>62447249
Ehhhhhhhzz . I watched. It. Only gay bears would hate. Kek.

62447259
>>62447256
omg you are a fucking RACIST!

62447272
No 1pbtid, bears fucked
I’m long as donkey Kong has on eth
Many florets of spring

item.title: War Service (Charlebois Family) — 6 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
War Service (Charlebois Family)

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-05 07:45 UTC

OP

Ernest:

Great War, US Army, Cook; Blackjack Pershing.

Post-War, Detective Comics, Given to German Pennsylvania; Attleboro Carpet, Boat Delivered to Atlantic City casinos.

Raymond:

World War 2, USMC MI-6; Aircraft Mechanic, Howard Hughes.

Post-War, Paperclip; Integration, African Tribals and German Defectors, merged into anti-Israeli community.

Mike Raytheon, CIA, shutdown of INTERPOL Rupee market, Taiwan and India; sales of "orphans", as vice prostitutes and corrections scholars.

Post-War, Disney World, hotels and valves, per showers and hotel rooms.

Dave: NSA, removals of Catholic Church as global organized crime, art houses, "Doge"; placement of American Pope on Pulpit, per legal charges explained.

Post-War, Ayatollah bombed; "Budd"; "Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair", "Christopher Nolan", "Arkham Asylum", "Gotham", and "Kingpin".

Early replies (first 3)

62447199
>>62447197
Finally, the Tactical Fund I was looking for

62447210
Kaiser Wilhelm: German farming techniques, outside of Native American top soil rotation and production of rare field goods.

Hirohito: Control of weapons and purchases through Japanese guns smiths.

Mitterand: Shipment of training dossiers and documents within stolen children for sexual gratification of homosexual NCOs.

Khameini: Movement against public school, literature, and poverty movements, of printed text; "Che" Guevera instead, beef diet against pork and swine.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447243
Lt. Field Officer:

Weimar Agent.
Vatican Assassin.
National Security Agency Signals Intelligence.
National Security Agency Human Intelligence.
MKULTRA Central Intelligence Agency.
Air Force Reserve Officer Training Corps.
Sicilian Gestapo.
Lieutenant Central Intelligence Officer.
Federal Bureau of Investigation.
State Police New Hampshire Jew Hunter.
Canadian House of York (COBRA).
Ministry of Security Services.
Vatican Honor Guard.

62447248
>>62447197
Death to the jew devil! Financially speaking ofc.

62447262
>>62447197
Help me understand what are you talking about here.
How can we profit with this knowledge?

item.title: Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux? — 9 replies — 6.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-05 07:00 UTC

OP

Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux?

How can you sell this old White girl, you should be ashamed. Take a good hard look in the mirror right NOW!

(I am not home atm, so at least I won’t be shoah’d from 4chan again for this thread for another week on my home internet)

Early replies (first 3)

62444972
>>62444945
i love she

62445037
>>62444945
She wouldn't lick the peanutbutter of my balls so she had to go, I'm sorry.

62445045
>>62445037
the comments matches the color id

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445079
i used to have a west highland terrior. she was a good dog, but loved rolling around in other dog's shit. very annoying.

62445088
>>62445079
west highland terriers are predators, they will roll in shit or remains of dead animals to hide their scent like little rambo's

62446550
>>62444945
Make a TG faggot.

62446560
I will be honest. received an airdrop. Made me 20k. Ever since, I bought again. Do you think I care about such pocket change? Or do you think I understand how to winmax you retards? And next time that guy s around forewarn me.

62446579
>>62446560
Holy esl your skin is brown

62447209
>>62446550

item.title: stocking picking help! — 4 replies — 2.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
stocking picking help!

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-05 06:58 UTC

OP

I need an energy stock in my portfolio!
Currently have an order placed on BEPC!
Thing is... I am searching for long term growth (5 - 10 years). I also dont want a pure American company operating only in America, since I have been trying to reduce the geographical risk of my portfolio.

Aside from BEPC, I am considering:
>Enel / Iberdrola
>GEV
>CEG
>SSE
>Orsted
>RWE AG


Can anyone suggest me the highest growth, non-pure-American stock in this sector?

Early replies (first 3)

62445591
>>62445566
thread theme:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDS9gmdHtB8&list=RDCDS9gmdHtB8&start_radio=1&t=208s

62445994
Hungarian oil company MOL.
It still under valued because of the russian collaboration and profiteering on the sactioned oil.
The new hungarian minister of economic was a shell top manager.
Also the region will be pumped if the russian 3 days long special operation is over.

62447157
>>62445994
Is the ticker MOL?
I cant find it on yahoo.finance
I know KAZPROM.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447206
>>62447157
MOL.BD

You need access to the Budapest stock exchange, all the other ones have absolute shit volume.

item.title: UK bros - HMRC and crypto — 4 replies — 2.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UK bros - HMRC and crypto

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-05 06:54 UTC

OP

Do you have any experience with HMRC and crypto?

Anyone received nudge letters or notice to pay taxes on crypto??

All banking institutions are reporting money transfers to exchanges and HMRC are apparently coming after everyone.

Early replies (first 3)

62445035
Yes
If you ever interacted with a KYC exchange, they reported you to HMRC. I got the letter and paid up like a good goy.

62445038
>>62445035
Was it a nudge letter or a tax notice?

62445040
>>62445038
Nudge I guess. The CEX would have had my on chain transaction history so I didn't see the point in not paying and then later on being screwed for late tax payment + 7% interest on it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447201
>>62445040
How many years back did you have to go?

How did you calculate, via an accountant or Koinly?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-05 06:29 UTC

OP

XRP/BTC

YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED (not financial advice)

Early replies (first 3)

62445379
>>62445330
Just admit what? You guys are wrong on every single accusation and every single accusation is Alinsky projection. You talk about tokens not being needed etc. You guys don't even understand your own investment thesis and when you have it clarified you throw a fucking FIT that is on par with antinatalist leftists seeing a happy traditional white family have children. You have a resentment based group identity that is REALLY cringily built around a fucking coin specific greed cult. Even when you have it proven before you you deny and deflect and project and throw the most vile and venemous knowingly wrong shit you can imagine with zero integrity or even remotely human treatment, being in the same price as 6 years ago, and being able to pivot and just move and reposition based on what you know to be true. You care less about what is true than you care about just posturing to have your coin be shown to be favorable even knowing your coin is literally just a liquidity extraction channel to be a paypig for a company whose builtout product does not translate back to token value. It is so weird it is almost tought to imagine existing let alone being. I am SO fucking happy to not be you I can't even fucking imagine the Hell it would be...

62445399
>>62445330
Both are f**ked...should've bought LTC

62445502
>>62445379
t. seething linkie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447174
>>62445502
100%. He is REALLY mad he has nothing to argue and that everyone who tries gets rekt but can't cope without being loud despite having nothing to say so he copy pastes my old posts from the archives and sometimes inserts random shit projects like fucking BSV shilling into it. Really unimaginable how he thinks this is a meaningful proof against XRP or how it is not extremely obvious admission to himself and everyone that he cannot defend link and is a literal cult devotee committed to messaging games for his findom master over actually making it.

62447178
>>62447174

item.title: BREAKING [siren emoji]: XRP is so fucking bullish right now — 5 replies — 2.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
BREAKING [siren emoji]: XRP is so fucking bullish right now

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-05 06:16 UTC

OP

BREAKING [siren emoji]: XRP is so fucking bullish right now

Early replies (first 3)

62445415
>>62445403
1. BTC
2. XRP
3. ETH
4. DOGE
5. SOL
6. TRX
7. USDT
8. BNB
9. LTC
10. QNT

62445457
>>62445403
>>62445415
Literally who gives a fuck. You guys always spam this. SWIFT is literally no exaggeration the financial equivalent of the fax machine. Or Google up until recently with their "moat" around their search business. Then AI comes and it is all over. Lol XRP and crypto in general is that. GPI is WAY too little WAY too late and CCIP is literally hundreds of times more expensive and slower than XRP using ILP. It isn't even remotely close. If banks choose to stay they choose to underperform available technologies by 100x and pay the premium to do so in a more centralized way for no benefit and more risk. Meanwhile community banks which choose otherwise will literally be able to outperform JP Morgan in international remittances. Institutions which don't join will be left in the dust. If you introduce speciation and give one series 1% advantage over the other in 100 years it will have driven it into near extinction. It's just math, and Tom ENDLESSLY seethes on Twitter and Linkein but never really says or refutes anything. He is currently seething that SWIFT were not invited to Ripple's Swell and talking about how Sibos is so great because anyone can attend (but Ripple chose not to, I wonder why!). It's almost like its a dying legacy system that will increasingly be eat into even outside Ripple by Target and newly emergent payment systems that no longer require its 70 year old messaging software and have no benefit from its massively uncompetitive attempt to cope with disruptive tech. I am whiter than you and for that reason I will never hold link.

62445607
>>62445403
When was this image made OP? Mine is from just now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445707
>>62445403
>>62445415
>>62445457
>>62445607

62447163
>>62445457
JSYK this is a linkie who just copypastes old posts of mine from the archive, sometimes inserting to shill a different project. I have genuinely no idea how he thinks this is an own and not just obviously really weird cope. Lol
>>62445707
You are replying to one of your own low IQ cult members lil nigga

OP

a literal fucking mossad netanyahu laundering operation LOL!!!!! no wonder they always refuse to pay out when you win and everythings rigged.

62444716
>>62444709

62444718
>>62444716

62444725
>>62444709
Cattle who gamble in a casino they don’t own deserve to be taken advantage of

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445007
>>62444992
>>62444993
Full of compliant jobless people. The cities have all the power, internet and water infrastructure so they are obviously the most valuable real estate. Your idea of a revolution will not play out. It's funny in january i was arguing with a scitard about how data centers would be built in places they shouldn't but he kept trying to come up with counter examples like them using ocean instead and I told him they would always take a short cut, now look at what's happening today. You are using an outdated model of the world to reason about this. You simply don't understand how fast everything is moving? These people can put up automated turrets at every building and monitor traffic in real time if they wanted to. Stop believing hollywood slop about people rising up. It won't work. They have tools that can do surveillance at a scale that you can't imagine. They just need a crisis to deploy them at full power. Whatever story or film you watched that promised you revolution is outdated and obsolete. You need to update your thinking because things are moving too fast now.

62445032
>>62444709

62445034

62445252

The ftx jew the same story except in the end he wasn't important enough to keep out of jail. It's always them

62445672

62446511

62447076
>>62447071

62447079
>>62446511

62447223
>>62447079
do you understand how laundering works anon? when you get a win in a casino your winnings are taxed and now legal with legitimate paper trail. even if you dont rig the outcome you can split your bet between both options of a 50:50 guess so one is guaranteed to pay out and whatever you lose is just the cost of making money clean.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 202 replies — 31.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 202 • Updated: 2026-07-05 01:59 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

62431586
bump.

62431599
probably nothing
bio protocol desci ai BIO now cookin with open ai, nvidia, anthropic, claude news check twita

62432055
>>62373752
Hey
I just started out in this crypto thing and bought $450 worth of solana. How do I become cool and multiply it

62432088
>>62432055
Be like me and listen to people within a group of other retards that are pushing a token. The last one tanked a $200 investment -98% but then he re-deployed the same token and it yielded a 40x from where it was called.

62432306
>>62432088
pardon my incompetence but I didn't understand much of what you just wrote anon

62432350
>>62428797
I had bad experiences following twitter people and waiting for signals even those that seem smart. It can be interesting but you’ll be lost if they randomly stop posting, they’re not funds giving you alpha but people that should have better things to do than post if their full job is actually trading.

I do still like @donalt and @cryptocred their show technical roundup is a rare non clickbaity show that just focuses on TA but most people are inherently frauds on social media

62432360
>>62432055
I don’t think Solana is a good buy right now it’s barely holding support and likely going lower to like 40 if I remember correctly

>>62432088
If you have a group like that be a hero to the community and share it to us

62432373
>>62432360
>be a hero to the community and share it to us
see
>>62385756
I recently held an airdrop for /biz/ too

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62424715

62434369
Bump, I'm getting an early weekend because of the country's birthday. Will devote time to this thread.

62435112
>>62432350
skill issue. donalt? lol
you gotta follow the small accounts anon

62436077
>>62435112
Why would you rely on random accounts calls and not build the skill yourself? You are just fucked if you only listen to others, and you have no way to know they aren’t just lucky for a while until they drag you down with themselves

>donalt
Yes he may be old and washed but you can still learn a lot from two traders that never shilled some memecoin. Except for ftx but I guess everyone had an ftx sponsorship for a while

62436265
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD
>200 REPLIES

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOO OH NO NO NO NO AAAAAAAHHHHHH

62437580
>>62436077
did you read my first post? i literally said not to just blindly copy trade
there's nothing wrong with using great traders as confluence

62440778
bamp.

62442413
Bump

62443230
Man i wanna move to SLC someday

62444221
>>62443230
Why

62446809
I’m going to college and bc I graduated mitary style I have someone else saving half and half, even if my shortcomings are just too coming for me to live my best life I get to shoot

62446811
>>62446809
I’m also t50

item.title: Why are younger people encouraged to make greater risks when it comes to investing? — 63 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why are younger people encouraged to make greater risks when it comes to investing?

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-07-05 06:08 UTC

OP

Why are younger people encouraged to make greater risks when it comes to investing? Doesn't it make much more sense to invest in a low risk ETF while you're younger and have more time? Why should some 22 year old who just learned about the stock market yesterday put all their money into what's essentially gambling as opposed to VOO or some shit that's bound to go up?

Early replies (first 3)

62444847
>bro just invest for the next 50 years so u can and a golden nest egg at 70 years old maybe

Look at the demographics

Look at the browning of the population

Imagine thinking the last 50 years of growth is gonna happen again in the next 50 years

You should be doing everything in your power to make it NOW, not LATER

62444848
>>62444837
because they've got 50 years of waging ahead of them, so greater risk for greater reward makes sense, given they will be able to get through any hard times unscathed due to aforementioned bulk years of earning potential remainig. low risk investing is for old cunts who have limited earning potential remaining, or have retired already.

62444851
>>62444837
Speaking as someone who helped advertise crypto in the second btc bullcycle, younger people are easier marks. They tend to be idealistic and there's a lot of dumb money among trust fund kids, techbros, people who did well with crypto once and thing they have a magic touch, etc. Old people are nervous.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446186
>>62446183
meant >>62446156 but I'm retarded and posting this from a phone

62446202
>>62446183
>portfolio
This nu-male nu-age speak is sterile, a man ought to have a stack, booty, treasure trove, a good ol hoard

62446269
>>62446202
Everyone with a "stack" is broke and depressed

62446271
>>62446269
Loser cope

62446281
>>62446183
By 40 you should be rich and retired. Your only concern is not fucking up the paradise you built.

Do the heavy lifting when you are young live an easy life 40 plus thats what I did

62446287
>>62446281
Living the life eating cheap canned dogfood lmao

62446293
>>62446281
> not fucking up the paradise you built
That is indeed what should be done. People in communist suits in plain sight like Merkel make it even the more obvious! I am not even kidding.

62446313
>>62446293
She was a commie they are like bitter poors like this clown>>62446287 they can only destroy

62446317
>>62446313
>oy vey stop insulting my canned dogfood goyim

62446429
>>62446317
Wtf? I understood his reply. Yours in direction at me, is something you apologize for.

62446457
>>62444837
>Why are younger people encouraged to make greater risks when it comes to investing?

Who say that? DCA and pray is the only way

62446471
>>62446429
Lol retard

62446489
>>62446471
You need to go back is all.

62446512
/biz/ is proving it's fucking retarded and doesn't understand finance as always

>>62446016
Only correct post in this thread and I'll expand on it. In an efficient market risk is compensated by a higher average return. Due to the increased volatility you need a longer time frame on your risky investment to realise this. Therefore younger people who have more time ahead of them are better positioned to benefit from the increased gains of risky benefits compared to boomers who need their money within the next couple of years.

For example, a treasury gives a lower average return than the s&p index. But it is a much less volatile and you can basically guarantee it grows each year, whereas there will be years where you lose money on the stock market index.

62446546
>>62446005
If you are not a lazy worthless nigger, sure getting old sucks. There's a guy at my gym, 80 years old and he can squat 315 lbs. More than you can do at your current age. Health is wealth, and if you take care of yourself, getting old isn't that bad.

62446580
>>62444837
because the stock market as a whole barely beats inflation long-term

that's why even after investing for 55 years most people only end up with 15 years worth of living expenses saved up.

62446595
>>62446512
le /biz7 is le evi, we le commnist bros will save you all. You have 2 choices. 1. I annihilate you here in 4chins. Or 2 I do later. And there won#t be laws to make you stop dying

62446666
>>62446546
3 plate squatting at 80 years olds? hahaha fuck off you delusional faggot

>>62446580
WOAH LOOK SOMEONE WHO GETS IT, ONLY TOOK A DAY

62446674
>>62446666
Checked, but this is very clearly cope on your part. You can't fathom someone being 80 squatting 3 plates because you can't even do it.

62446680
>>62446674
Ive been lifting for over 20 years. No 80 year old is going to be squatting 3 plates.

62446798
>>62446680
You are larping just based on that sentence alone.

62446807
>>62446489
Get back in the oven

62446813
>>62446798
You're actually a jeet from some ghetto shithole who gets paid in cow dungs, prove me wrong (you cant) geg

62447086
>>62444837
Because the old harvests the life force of the young. What better way than to trick them into thinking 'why not, you've got time'.

>>62445968
And yet the thing people want most in the world is to be young again. Young people have far more to lose than society lets on. Their entire life will be spent working for the old, until they themselves are old and wishing they were young again, and thus their harvest of the next generation will start.

It is sickening to watch. You have a period of time of about 12 years to get your shit together including your finances to determine the outcome of the rest of your life and the old tries to get you loaded up with as much debt as possible during this time so that they can steal decades of your life force.

You work for the old, because the old cant work for the young retard.

The old already work for the young, You get 18 good years, the fact that you wasted them is not our fault.

The truth is the rich are the ones that make you slave away not the old, the old are just as much slaves as you will be. Get a job, you really don't need to do much work to contribute enough to society if you play your cards right (and don't scam people). or you can find joy in work and making people happy. Or you can sell your soul for a office daycare job.

Or just start your own company, oh wait you should have spent your time learning instead of just consuming brain rot and being a useless consumer and not a creator.
